facts.statement: Mastercard Inc is listed on the NYSE under ticker MA and is classified in the SEC submissions metadata under SIC description "Services-Business Services, NEC."

Mastercard says its global acceptance footprint serves more than 3.7 billion accounts and connects hundreds of millions of acceptance locations and digital access points across more than 220 countries and territories.

Mastercard describes its expertise as encompassing threat intelligence, economic analysis, cyber security, loyalty programming, open finance, fraud prevention, and payments including agentic commerce and digital assets.
Mastercard identifies three strategic priority areas: core payments, commercial and new payment flows, and value-added services and solutions.
Mastercard's Payment Solutions segment reported net revenue of $32.791 billion in 2025, compared with $28.167 billion in 2024 and $25.098 billion in 2023.
Mastercard reported Consolidated Net Income of $14.968 billion in 2025, compared with $12.874 billion in 2024 and $11.195 billion in 2023.
The SEC company facts summary reports Mastercard operating cash flow of $11.980 billion for FY2025, filed February 11, 2026.
The SEC company facts summary reports Mastercard had $8.442 billion of cash and equivalents, $17.476 billion of long-term debt, and $6.485 billion of stockholders' equity for FY2025.
Mastercard identifies Payment Solutions as its segment, and its Chief Executive Officer is identified as the chief operating decision-maker for assessing segment performance and allocating resources.

The 10-K says the CODM decides whether to reinvest profits into the Payment Solutions segment or other business activities such as acquisitions, dividends, or share repurchases based on Consolidated Net Income.

Revenue generated in the United States was approximately 29% of Mastercard net revenue in 2025, 30% in 2024, and 30% in 2023; no individual country other than the U.S. generated more than 10% of net revenue in those periods.
Mastercard did not have any individual customer that generated more than 10% of net revenue in 2025, 2024, or 2023.
Mastercard says it facilitated more than 175 billion transactions in 2025, with gross dollar volume growing 9% and cross-border volume growth up 15% year over year on a local-currency basis.
Mastercard says commercial credit and debit represented 13% of gross dollar volumes in 2025 and grew 11% year over year on a local-currency basis.
Mastercard says it had tokenized nearly 40% of all switched transactions as of December 2025 and has a goal of reaching 100% tokenization of e-commerce payments by 2030.
Mastercard says every transaction that touches its network generates data protected by Mastercard, and it cited "15+ petabytes of structured, clean, usable data."
Mastercard describes the Mastercard Move network as transmitting value across 17 billion endpoints.
Michael Miebach signed the 2025 Form 10-K as President and Chief Executive Officer and director, Sachin Mehra signed as Chief Financial Officer, and Sandra Arkell signed as Corporate Controller.

The 10-K trading-arrangements disclosure identifies Ed McLaughlin as President & Chief Technology Officer, Mastercard Technology; Ling Hai as President, Asia Pacific, Europe, Middle East & Africa; and Craig Vosburg as Chief Services Officer.
Mastercard says Consolidated Net Income and other measures are used for internal planning and forecasting and in the calculation of performance-based compensation.

Mastercard's 2026 proxy states the Board nominated 11 directors, recommended votes for all 11 nominees, and had received a contingent resignation from each nominee under its majority-voting policy for uncontested elections.

Mastercard's bylaws permit a stockholder or group of up to 20 stockholders owning 3% or more of outstanding Class A common stock for at least three years to submit director nominees for up to 20% of the Board for inclusion in the proxy statement, subject to bylaw requirements.

Mastercard recorded 2025 pre-tax litigation provisions of $504 million, primarily related to a change in estimate for merchants who opted out of U.S. merchant class litigation, a U.S. liability shift litigation provision, and a provision associated with ATM non-discrimination rule surcharge complaints.

Mastercard's President and Chief Executive Officer and Chief Financial Officer concluded disclosure controls and procedures were effective as of December 31, 2025, and PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP audited the consolidated financial statements and internal control over financial reporting.
